L1.area: Network communication and connection handling layer

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code by sending crafted serialized Java objects over the network. The application deserializes incoming objects from network streams without restricting which classes can be instantiated, enabling remote code execution through malicious payloads.

L3.area: Network connector and connection establishment logic within the communication subsystem
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the network connection handling logic where incoming serialized Java objects are read from network socket streams without any class filtering or validation during the connection handshake. Unauthenticated remote attackers can send crafted serialized payloads to achieve arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The Connector class deserializes Java objects from unauthenticated network connections using ObjectInputStream without any ObjectInputFilter, allowing remote attackers to send malicious serialized objects for arbitrary code execution.
